rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEY JOHNSON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 4, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence based on factually devoid discovery responses. The identification of Easterlin in the amended interrogatory responses, absent any statement as to what he knew, is insufficient to preclude shifting the burden to plaintiff. Defendant's objections to plaintiff exhibits A and D are sustained based on hearsay. The Easterlin declaration, therefore, is not relevant because it is based on the hearsay documents and is devoid of evidence of any contact with decedent. The Brown declaration is admissible to establish defendant's boilers were on the ships but it does not establish exposure by decedent to asbestos. The Ay declaration is based on inadmissible hearsay and for that reason, it is not admitted. Plaintiff has failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR WEBB VS. UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 4, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff is challenging the denial of Employment Development Department benefits. The benefits were denied in case #A0-138442 by defendant on October 27, 2006 and plaintiff did not take a writ pursuant to CCP section 1094.5, from that decision. The decision by Judge Berger in case #1942825 only allowed plaintiff to reopen that case, not the prior case (#1892564/A0-138442). Plaintiff has not sought a writ from any action taken by defendant in case #1942825. Plaintiff has not plead factors demonstrating that he can maintain a claim against defendant or that any such claim would not be barred by the 6 month statute of limitations applicable to CCP section 1094.5. See Sinetos v. DMV (1984) 160 Cal. App. 3d 1172, 1176. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ELEANOR JENKINS VS. WAYNE BELL et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 4, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANT DON WARD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to causes of action for fraud, misrepresentation and elder abuse. Sustained without leave to amend; no opposition filed as to constructive fraud and constructive trust. Plaintiff to plead facts describing how it is Bell was given plaintiff's power of attorney in 2003; plaintiff to attach or describe the terms of Bell's power of attorney; plaintiff to plead facts related to the transfer of the property to All Saints and plaintiff's knowledge, if any, of the transfer; plaintiff to plead the fair market value of the property and the amount paid by Ward for the property; and plaintiff to plead, with greater precision, when Bivens learned the property was for "sale". =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: LEO KAISER VS. VANYA GOLDBERG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 4, 2008, line 11. DEFENDANT VANYA GOLDBERG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendants failed to shift the burden of producing evidence as to grounds 1 and 3. Defendant's did no as to 2 (nuisance) and to the extent plaintiff's claim is based on CCP 1161 (1), it fails as a matter of law. The motion is denied as to the 3 day notice to quit for habitual late payment. such a notice is authorized by CCP 1161 (3) and the cure language is not fatal to plaintiff's claim. See Richard v. Degen & Brody, Inc. (1960) 181 Cal. App. 2d 289; Rutter Group, Landlord-tenant section 7:137. The motion is granted as to the notice regarding the nuisance which does not comply with SFRO section 37.9(a)(3). Plaintiff's causes of action based on CCP 1161 (3) may be maintained. Plaintiff also provided adequate notices as to each covenant re: habitual late rent, weeding and destruction of landscaping. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: 1095 MARKET STREET HOLDING, LLC VS. JOSEPH THOMAS et al
